def: s.f. A rubber; a towel; a brush, broom, besom; — also = mārjan, q.v.

ety: rel. n. fr. marjān
def: adj. Of coral, coral-.
s.m. = S مارجاري मार्जारी mārjārī, s.f. A cat; the wild cat; the civet-cat; the pole-cat.
s.f. = S مارجار मार्जार mārjār, s.m. A cat; the wild cat; the civet-cat; the pole-cat.
s.f. corr. of مرضي marẓī, q.v.

ety: S. मृता+जीव+र+कः
def: adj. = mariyal, q.v.

ety: S. मज्ज+इन्+कः
def: s.m. A diver.

ety: pass. part. of رجع 'to return,' &c.
def: part. Returned; referred; brought back; brought (into court, as a suit).

ety: for A. مرجوعة marjūʻat, fem. of marjūʻ
def: part. = marjūʻ, q.v.
s.m. The wind in the body, the vital air which pervades the body; — the body; — a monkey; — an imp.

ety: n. of place fr. رقب 'to look, to watch,' &c.
def: s.m. A high place of observation; a watch-tower.

ety: n. of place fr. رکب 'to ride' (upon
def: s.m. Anything upon which one rides, or upon or in which one is borne or carried; a beast, a horse, a camel; a ship, vessel, boat; a saddle; a vehicle (syn. sawārī).

ety: cf. S. मट्टकं
def: s.m. Ridge (of a house).

ety: n. of place, fr. رقد 'to sleep'
def: s.f. A sleeping-place, a bed; — a sepulchre, tomb, grave.

ety: mar ( = mār, q.v.)+S. कार+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) Addicted to beating or striking; addicted to butting

ety: comp. past conj. part. of marnā; — mar = mari = Prk. मरिअ = S. मृत्वा+kar = kari = Prk. करिअ = S. कृत्वा
def: part. Having died; — adv. Dead; — with great labour or difficulty
s.f. A hollow, hole, cavity, chasm; — a vessel, pot; — a barren woman.
s.m. (f. -ī), A monkey, an ape; — a spider; — a large crane, Ardea argala; — a kind of venom.
s.m. A kind of ebony, Diospyros melanoxylon.

ety: S. मर्कटिका
def: s.f. A female monkey; — a small monkey.

ety: n. of place fr. رکز 'to stick, or fix' (into
def: s.m. A fixed station; a centre; centre (of a circle); — upper stroke (of the letters kāf and gāf)
subs: v.t. To assume (a point) to be the centre (of a circle), to take (a point) as the centre
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرکز (markaz), itself from Arabic مَرْكَز (markaz).

ety: comp. conj. part. of marnā; and = mar, q.v.+ke = कय् = कर = करि, q.v.
def: adv. = mar-kar, q.v.
subs: v.n. To have a narrow escape from death; to recover from severe illness.

ety: mar ( = mār, q.v.)+S. कार+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) Addicted to beating or striking; addicted to butting

ety: mar ( = mār, q.v.)+S. कार+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) Addicted to beating or striking; addicted to butting
s.m. (dialec.) The rim of a well.

ety: mark˚, prob. fr. S. मृष्; nā, as in ḍubonā, q.v.
def: v.t. (dialec.) To sprinkle (water, on grain, &c.), to wet, damp.

ety: pass. part. of رقم 'to mark; to figure,' &c.
def: part. adj. Marked; written, inscribed; described; computed; dated; above-mentioned, aforesaid
subs: or marqūma-bālā, adj. Above-written, above-mentioned, aforesaid

ety: for A. مرقومة maṛqūmat, fem. of marqūm
def: part. adj. Marked; written, inscribed; described; computed; dated; above-mentioned, aforesaid
subs: or marqūma-bālā, adj. Above-written, above-mentioned, aforesaid

ety: pass. part. of رکز 'to stick, or fix' (into
def: part. Fixed; — infixed; implanted; — hid, concealed; buried (in); — comprehended; concentred; — s.m.(?) Fixed desire (of); desire, wish; design.

s.m. A vital part (of the body), vulnerable or mortal spot; tender place; weak organ, or part, or point (in); — any vital member or organ; the core (of anything); the quick; a joint; — anything which requires to be kept concealed, secret foible; secret design or purpose; hidden meaning; — anything hidden or recondite; secret recess (of the heart, &c.), the innermost heart; — a secret, mystery; truth
subs: or marma-vedī, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, and inī), Knowing weak or vulnerable points; knowing secrets or the hidden circumstances (of any affair); knowing mysteries, familiar with the most secret or recondite portions of a subject, deeply versed (in); any very acute or learned man; a Brāhmaṇ; a spy
or maram-sthān, s.m. A vital part, vulnerable place; a tender spot, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. marma.

ety: Gr. μάρμαρον, fr. μαρμαίρω, 'to shine'; Lat. marmor
def: s.m. Marble ( = sang-marmar).

ety: S. मर्मरः, onomat.; cf. also S. मुर्मुरः
def: s.m. & f. A rustling sound; rustling, murmuring, murmur; crackling, crackle; sound of crunching, &c.

ety: marmar or murmur, q.v. +ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To rustle; to murmur, mutter, grumble; to creak, to grate; to crackle, &c.
s.f. A species of pine tree Pinus deodora.

ety: S. मर्मिकः
def: adj. & s.m. = marma-gya, & marma-vedī, q.v.s.v. marma or maram.

ety: mār˚ = Prk. मार(इ), or मारे(इ) = S. मारय(ति), caus. of rt. मृ;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To beat, strike, thump, smite; to hit, knock; to punish; — to kill, slay, slaughter; to destroy, ruin, mar, spoil, blight, blast; to crack, break; to deaden or blunt (the edge of a knife, &c.); to destroy the active qualities of, to kill (quicksilver, lead, &c.), to calcine; — to strike out; to thrust out; — to attack (villages, houses, or travellers); to rob or seize with assault (i.e. with killing, or personal violence), to plun der; to embezzle; to withhold wrongfully; — to earn smartly and cleverly; to make (profit in business); — to stop, close; to fine; — to overcome, conquer, vanquish, defeat; to seize, take, capture, catch; to master subdue. tame, mortify (affections, appetites, or lusts); to calm, quench, allay, suppress, smother; to counteract (poison, &c.); — to press down; to stamp; — to drive; to drive or ram in; to insert; to apply, fix, fasten, clap on; — to set; to throw, to pelt with, to cast (a spell); to toss; to shoot, discharge, fire (a gun), dart or hurl (a missile), lay on (a sword, &c.); to fight (a battle); to strike or make (a sudden assault); to strike up (a run, dance, strain, &c.); to strike out (the hands or legs); to pitch (a tent); to chuck in, or toss off (a morsel); to raise or set up (a cry); to make or do (generally with vehemence, eagerness, or smartness); — (as a gerund) s.m. Striking, beating, &c.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To kill outright, to slay, to murder
v.t. (intens.) To beat completely, to overcome, conquer, &c.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀸𑀭𑁂𑀇 (mārei), from Sanskrit मारयति (mārayati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *māráyati, from Proto-Indo-European *moréyeti, from *mer- + *éyeti.

def: v.t. To rub; to thresh corn; to flatten, &c. ( = māṅḍnā, q.v.).

ety: mar˚ = Prk. मर(इ) = S. म्रिय(ते), but Vedic also मर(ति), rt. मृ
def: v.n. To die, expire; to cease; to be dead (as in a game); to fade, wither; to be lost (as a stake, or a bad debt); to become bankrupt or insolvent (in these senses mar-jānā is the more com. form); — to die or be dying (for, -par), to set the heart (upon), to be desperately enamoured (of), or in love (with), to desire vehemently; — to labour or toil hard (for, -ko), to sweat and groan, to suffer hardship; — (as a gerund) s.m. Dying, death
subs: v.n. To work (oneself) to death, to labour excessively; to suffer pain or sorrow
v.n. To die; to fade, &c. (see marnā); to die away, die off; to be as though dead; to swoon
v.n. To recover from death; — s.m. Escape from the jaws of death
v.n. To die; to be dead; to lie dead; — to be dying (for, -par), &c., see marnā
v.n. To die and be effaced; to be killed or slain; — to be ruined, be sacrificed (for, -ke pīćhe)
or marne-hārā, adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Doomed to die, dying, perishable, mortal; — a mortal
adj. Fit or deserving to die
About to die, at the point of death
To have no time to die; to be overwhelmed with work, to have too much to do to think of dying
To die prematurely; to die a violent death.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀭𑀇 (maraï), from Sanskrit मर॑ति (márati).

ety: prob. fr. the preceding
def: s.f. Paste; starch (see māṛī, and māṅḍī).

ety: S. स्मर+मोहः
def: s.f. Love, tenderness, affection, kindness, mercy, pity, compassion, humanity.

ety: maroh, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Affectionate, kind; merciful, compassionate; — humane; — an affectionate, or a compassionate, person.
s.f. (dialec.) = maroṛ, q.v.; — s.m. = maroli, q.v.
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: S. मरोलिकः
def: s.m. The sea-monster makar, q.v.
s.f. = مروڙ maroṛ, q.v.
s.f. = مروڙ maroṛ, q.v.

ety: fr. maroṛnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Turn, bend, flexion, twist, convolution, writhe, contortion (syn. akaṛ, q.v.)
subs: s.m. An affected person; a fop; a swaggerer
s.m. The screw-tree, Helicteres isora, and its fruit (which is used medicinally for gripes).

def: s.m. = مروڙا maroṛā, q.v.

ety: maroṛ+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. Twist; contortion ( = maroṛ); — twisting of the bowels, gripes, colic; flux; tenesmus: — māroṛā uṭhnā v.n. Twisting of the bowels to arise; gripes or colic to come on.
s.m. = مروڙا maroṛā, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of maroṛnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Twisted; bent, &c.
subs: s.f. A twisted or bent uail; — a screw.

ety: maroṛ, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका; — and also fem. of the part. maroṛā, q.v.
def: s.f. A twisting, contortion, writhing; — anything twisted; — a screw, metal worm or spindle
subs: To be twisted; to turn; to coil; — to be perplexed ( = peć khānā, q.v.s.v. peć).

ety: maroṛ+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. Yearning; affectionate (syn. marohī).
s.f. = مروڙي maroṛī, q.v.
v.t. = مروڙنا maroṛnā, q.v.

ety: maroṛ, prob. fr. intens. of rt. मृद्; or by redupl. fr. caus. of rt. मुर्; cf. muṛnā; moṛnā
def: v.t. To turn, bend, double up; to twist, writhe, contort, distort; to gripe; to yearn; — to squeeze to death; — to crunch up, to devour.
v.t. = مروڙنا maroṛnā, q.v. (and cf. moṛnā).

ety: fr. mar-paćnā, q.v.
def: adj. Worked to death; macerated; fermented; rotten, fetid.
v.n. See s.v. marnā.

ety: conj. part. of marnā and piṭnā
def: adv. = markar, q.v.

ety: for A. مرة marrat, n. of un. fr. marruṉ, inf. n. of مرّ 'to pass, pass by,' &c.
def: s.m. One time, or turn; — adv. Once.
v.n. See s.v. marnā.
s.f. pl. (of marṛat), Times, turns.

ety: S. मार्ष or मारिष+कः
def: s.m. A species of pot-herb, Amaranthus oleraceus (syn. māṭ or māṭh).

ety: corr. fr. Ar. misann, noun of instr. fr. سنّ 'to sharpen, to whet'
def: s.f. A small whetstone.
s.m. = marsā, q.v.

ety: for A. مرثية mars̤iyat, inf. n. of رثي 'to weep over, to bewail'
def: s.m. A lamentation for one dead, an elegy, a dirge, an epicedeum (esp. the one sung during the Moḥarram in commemoration of Hasan and Hosain)
subs: s.m. Reciter or chanter of a mars̤iya
s.f. Reciting or chanting of a mars̤iya.

ety: pass. part. of رسم 'to mark, to stamp,' &c.
def: part. Signed, marked, notified, sketched out, described; prescribed; — accustomed.

ety: imperf. part. of mārnā; and = Prk. मारंतओ; S. मारत् (अन्त+कः)
def: part. Beating, striking, &c. (see mārnā); — s.m. One who strikes or beats
subs: s.m. The one who can overcome, or cure, or teach a lesson (to), &c.; the match (for), the masterer (of), the man (or boy, or lad, for)
s.m. One who is addicted to striking, &c.; a tyrant, an oppressor.

ety: imperf. part. of marnā, q.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dying; — s.m. A dying man
subs: s.m. The time of death, the dying moments.

r: martaba, martabat, rutba, is martabe-tak, ek martaba, kamāl martaba
ety: v.n. fr. رتب 'to be or become firm, or steady,' &c.
def: adv. To (or in) perfection, perfectly, completely

s.m. A glazed (earthenware) jar (used for keeping preserves and pickles, &c. in); a vessel of the finest porcelain which poison cannot penetrate (also written mart̤abān; corrupt forms are maratbān and imratbān).
s.m. = مرتبان martabān, q.v.

r: martabat, martaba, rutba, is martabe-tak, ek martaba, kamāl martaba
ety: v.n. fr. رتب 'to be or become firm, or steady,' &c.
def: adv. To (or in) perfection, perfectly, completely

ety: S. मृतक + इल+इकः
def: adj. (dialec.) = mariyal, q.v.

ety: S. मृतक + इल+इकः
def: adj. (dialec.) = mariyal, q.v.
s.m. corr. fr. markat, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = martailiyā, and mariyal, qq.v.
s.m. The sun; — a hog.
adj. (f. -ā), Doomed to die, liable to death, mortal.
s.m. corr. of martya-lok, q.v.

ety: Portug. martello
def: s.m. A hammer; — a turnscrew.
s.m. (Dakh.) corr. of mārtol, q.v.
s.m. contrac. of mārtol, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of رطب 'to be or become moist,' &c.
def: adj. Moist, humid, wet, damp; full of humours; — supple, soft.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Doomed to die, liable to death, mortal; — a mortal, a man, a human being, a person; — the world of mortals, the earth; — the mortal frame, the body
subs: or martya-pur, s.m. The world (or habitation) of mortals, the earth, the present world.

ety: Prk. मारिउओ; S. मारितृ+कः
def: s.m.f. Striking, smiting; attacking; wounding, &c. (see mārnā); warlike; destructive, deadly, fatal; — striker, smiter, killer, &c. (generally as last member of compounds); — s.m. A kind of deadly weapon made of two antelope-horns tipped with steel, and joined at their bases; — (dialec.) lord, husband; dear one, love; — a species of egg-plant, Solanum diffusum, and its fruit; — mārū-baiṅgon, s.m. Idem.

ety: Prk. मड्डुओ; S. मड्डुकः
def: s.m. A kind of warlike musical instrument, a kettledrum
s.m. See مرو maru.

s.m. Desert, sandy desert, arid tract or region; dry ground, soil destitute of water; — a mountain, rock; — name of a king belonging to the Ikshvāku family; — name of the province of Mārwār, and of its people
subs: maru-bhūmi, s.f., or maru-deś, maru-sthal, s.m. A dry or desert tract of country, arid region; barren soil.

ety: S. मालव+कः; or i.q. mārū, 'striking'
def: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode; a class, of songs.
s.f. See s.v. مرو maru.

ety: pass. part. of عرف 'to know'
def: part. adj. Known, well-known; famous, noted, celebrated, notorious; — (in Gram.) active (voice); — s.m. The active voice (of a verb); — (in Alg.) a known quantity; — the letters و and ي are said to be maʻrūf when the former has the sound of ū in nūr, and the latter that of ī in pīr.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْرُوف (ma'rūf), borrowed from Arabic مَعْرُوف (maʕrūf), from عَرَفَ (ʕarafa).

ety: fr. أر‌ْش 'men, creatures'
def: part. Created, formed.
adj. See s.v. مرو maru.
s.m. Name of an angel (see s.v. hārūt).
s.m. Wind, air, breath; — god of the wind (one of a number of deities who are guardians of the North-west quarter)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मृत (mṛta). Doublet of مردہ (murdā).

ety: fr. maruta
def: adj. Of or belonging to the Maruts; proceeding from the Maruts; belonging to air or wind, &c.; — s.m. Air, wind; vital air, one of the three humours of the body; — a Marut; — burnt offering on the occasion of conception, &c.
s.m. 'Son of the Maruts, or son of the Wind'; a patronymic of Bhīm-sen; and of Hanumān.

ety: S. मड़कः, with w unserted
def: s.m. (dialec.) The cereal Eleusine coracana (syn. āgī).

ety: S. मरुवकः
def: s.m. Name of a strong-scented plant, Artemisia vulgaris; — the plant Ocimum pilosum; — sweet marjoram, Origanum majorana.

ety: pass. part. of عرض 'to present, or offer (to
def: part. Presented, offered, exposed; submitted; — related; — written, dated; — maʻrūẓ, s.m. Exposition, explanation, representation, petition (syn. ʻarẓī).

ety: for A. معروضة maʻrūẓat, fem. of maʻrūẓ
def: part. Presented, offered, exposed; submitted; — related; — written, dated; — maʻrūẓ, s.m. Exposition, explanation, representation, petition (syn. ʻarẓī).
s.m. (dialec.) A kind of fish.

ety: S. मालव+कः; or i.q. mārū, 'striking'
def: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode; a class, of songs.

ety: for A. مروة marwat
def: s.m. The mountain of Mecca (so called from its flints).
adj., corr. or dialec. form of marohī, q.v.

ety: mar(nā), and mār(nā)+waiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. One dying, or at the point of death; a corpse; — one who is addicted to beating or striking, a beater, striker (syn. markhāhā).
s.m. (dialec.) A cess taken on marriages.

ety: doub. caus. of marnā
def: v.t. To cause to be killed, to cause to be put to death; — (doub. caus. of mārnā), v.t. To cause to be beaten, &c.; — to suffer sodomy (the word gāṅṛ being usually expressed)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To cause to be put to death; — to dismiss a charge or complaint.
ex: You would've got me killed today for sure!
wety: Causative form of مرنا (marnā) with stem augmented by وا (vā)

ety: S. मारुवार, fr. मरु 'desert'
def: s.m. Name of a country lying to the north of Gujerāt, and west of Jainagar.

ety: māṛwāṛ, q.v.+S. ईयः
def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to Māṛwāṛ; belonging to, or coming from, Māṛwāṛ; — a native of Māṛwāṛ; — s.f. The language or dialect of Māṛwāṛ.
s.m. A pearl: — marwārīd-ě-nā-sufta An unbored pearl.

ety: S. मृत+पट्टः
def: s.m. Rent-free land given to the families of retainers killed in battle; — painting the face of the bridegroom with rolī, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of روي 'to relate, rehearse,' &c.
def: part. Related, recited, rehearsed; affirmed, alleged; told.
adj. & s.m. = māṛiyā, q.v.

s.f. Limit, boundary, border, verge, frontier, bounds, confines; landmark, end, termination, terminus; — the bounds or restraint of law, rule, or established usage; social code; custom; — keeping within proper limits, propriety of conduct, decorum, decency, orderliness or guardedness of deportment, steadiness, straightforwardness, rectitude; reverential or respectful demeanour; respect, reputation, rank, station, dignity, honour
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Observant of bounds or of propriety of conduct; observing the bounds of law or custom, &c.; — respectful, of deferential or modest deportment; — respectable, estimable, aristocratic, honourable, dignified.

def: s.f. Limit, boundary, border, verge, frontier, bounds, confines; landmark, end, termination, terminus; — the bounds or restraint of law, rule, or established usage; social code; custom; — keeping within proper limits, propriety of conduct, decorum, decency, orderliness or guardedness of deportment, steadiness, straightforwardness, rectitude; reverential or respectful demeanour; respect, reputation, rank, station, dignity, honour
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Observant of bounds or of propriety of conduct; observing the bounds of law or custom, &c.; — respectful, of deferential or modest deportment; — respectable, estimable, aristocratic, honourable, dignified.

def: adj. (f. -ā, or -ī) = maryād-wān, q.v.s.v. maryād.

r: maryam, mariyam, ḥaẓrat-ě-maryam, mom-kī mariyam, lit.
def: prop. n. Mary
subs: A delicate woman (lit. 'a Mary of wax,' gen. used ironically).

ety: S. मर्या; cf. maryād
def: s.m. Limit, border, boundary, frontier, confines; — region, district
subs: (whence the Ar. marzubān), s.m. General of the confines, governor of the frontier, lord-marcher, margrave, landgrave; a satrap; a prince
or marz-o-būm, s.m. Limits or boundary (of a country), confines; region, district; empire, territory; — any place where one resides

ety: for A. مرزبة marzabat, 'the government of a satrap,' fr. marzu-bān, the Ar. form of the P. marz-bān, q.v.s.v. marz
def: s.m. Way, mode; — regimen, government.
s.m. See s.v. marz.

ety: pass. part. of رضي (orig. رضو 'to be pleased,' &c.
def: part. adj. Found pleasing, well-pleasing; liked, approved; agreeable, acceptable (syn. pasandīda); in which one takes a pleasure; — taking favourably; — s.f. Anything in which one takes a pleasure; — pleasure; assent, consent; choice; will, volition
subs: or marẕī-muwāfiq, adv. To the will or pleasure (of, -kī), satisfactorily
or marẓī paṭnā, (-kī), To agree, be of one mind; to suit
(-kī), To meet the wishes (of), to prove agreeable (to); to like
adv. Of (one's own) free will and consent, voluntarily.

ety: pl. of marẓī
def: s.f. Things agreeable.

ety: acc. of marẓīyat, fem. of marẓī
def: adv. Willingly; to (one's) will or pleasure, satisfactorily.

ety: Prk. मासं; S. मांसं (Vedic मास्)
def: s.m. Flesh, meat
s.m. 1˚ = māsh, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of māćh, q.v.

ety: Prk. मासओ; S. मासकः
def: s.m. A month ( = mās, and used chiefly as last member of compounds, e.g. ćau-māsā).
s.m. 1˚ = māshā, q.v.; — 2˚ = mās or māṅs, q.v.

ety: S. मस (rt. मस्)+कः
def: s.m. A certain land-measure, three-fourths of a bīghā.
s.f. Evening.

ety: Prk. मसओ; S. मशकः
def: s.m. A gnat, a musquito.

ety: S. मशकः
def: s.m. A gnat, musquito (i.q. masā, q.v.).

s.m. The moon (cf. Pers. māh); — a month, the twelith part of the Hindū year (usually, a lunar month consists of thirty Tithis; there may be also a solar month, equal to the sun's passage through a sign of the zodiac; a Sāvana month, consisting of thirty risings and settings of the sun; a Nakshatra month, regulated by the lunar asterisms, and a Bārhaspatya month, depending on the motions of the planet Jupiter; — the lunar month, being of two kinds as reckoned from the new or full moon, completes six modes of monthly computation)
subs: (˚sa+an˚), s.m. The last day of the (solar or lunar) month, the day of new moon

s.m. The last day of a month; — a monthly statement or abstract (of cases decided in a court, &c.; — according to Shakespear, this word is corr. fr. the Portuguese mes-acobar; but, more prob., it is a corr. of māsik-wār, or mās-kā wār)
s.m. Interest added to capital and subject to compound interest
s.f. An intercalary month; — monthly interest.

def: s.m. A gnat; a musquito (i.q. masā, q.v.).
s.m. A reel; a reed (used by weavers to wind their thread upon).

ety: S. मांस+कः; cf. masbird
def: s.m. A fleshy excrescence, a wart; — sight (of a gun)
subs: adj. Having warts, warty; (in Bot.) warted, papillose, verrucose.

def: s.m. = P مثابت mas̤ābat, s.f. (for A. مثابة mas̤ābat, n. of place, fr. ثوب 'to return,' &c.), A place to which one returns time after time; place of assembly or congregation; place of alighting or abode; a station; an inn, a caravansera.

s.f. = P مثابه mas̤āba, s.m. (for A. مثابة mas̤ābat, n. of place, fr. ثوب 'to return,' &c.), A place to which one returns time after time; place of assembly or congregation; place of alighting or abode; a station; an inn, a caravansera.
s.m. pl. (of maṣdar, q.v.), Sources; (in Gram.) infinitive nouns, nomina verbi or actionis.
(for A. مسافة, n. of place fr. ساف (for سوف) 'to smell'), s.f. lit. 'Place of smelling'; (hence) distance, space, interval (between); — a journey; — fatigue, labour.

ety: pl. of maṣaff, n. of place fr. صفّ 'to place or station in rows, ranks, or lines'
def: s.f. 'Places where ranks are drawn up in war or battle'; ranks (of an army); field (or fields) of battle; — battle.

ety: for A. مساحة misāḥat, inf. n. of مسح 'to measure'
def: s.f. Measuring; measurement; sarvey (of land); dimension; mensuration
s.m. pl. of muṣḥaf, q.v.), Books, volumes; Qorʼāns.

ety: S. मश+हर (rt. हृ)+इका
def: s.f. A bed-curtain for protection against musquitoes, musquito-curtain.
s.m. pl. (of muṣībat, q.v.), Shocks of fortune, afflictions, calamities.
s.m. pl. (of maśala, q.v.), Questions; propositions; problems; — the precepts of Moḥammad.
s.f. pl. (of masjid, q.v.), Places of worship, mosques.
s.m. A gnat, a musquito (i.q. masā, q.v.); — a kind of cutaneous eruption (in the form of dark, bean-like pustules).
s.f. corr. of mashk, or maśak, qq.v.; — s.m. corr. of maśak, 'a gnat,' q.v. (cf. masā).

ety: fr. masaknā, q.v.
def: s.f. Pinching and squeezing.

ety: masā, prob. fr. S. मृश्
def: adv. (dialec.) Barely, hardly, scarcely.

ety: for A. مساکة, inf. n. of مسك 'to hold tightly, to clutch'
def: s.f. Avarice, stinginess, penuriousness.
s.m. pl. (of maskan, q.v.), Habitations, mansions.
s.m. & f. pl. (of miskīn, q.v.), The poor, &c.

ety: masak˚ = S. मर्श (rt. मृश्)+कृ
def: v.t. To touch, rub, stroke, handle; — to press, squeeze

ety: masak˚ prob. = S. मष+कृ
def: v.n. To be torn or rent; to tear; to split, burst; to fall asunder, or in pieces; — to be very tight (a dress)

ety: masak˚ = S. मस्क(ते), rt. मस्क्
def: v.n. To move, stir.

ety: v.n. of مثل 'to compare, liken'
def: s.f. Fable, tale; parable, apologue; proverb, adage, proverbial saying; — example, instance, case in point; — comparison, simile, allegory, metaphor; — post, station; department
subs: To bring a proverb into use, to apply or to employ a proverb; — to adduce as an example

def: s.f. corr. of مثل mas̤al, q.v.
s.f. corr. of مشعل mashʻal, q.v.
adv. = mas̤alaṉ, q.v.
s.m. Materials, ingredients, &c. (corr. of مصالح maṣālěḥ, q.v.).
s.m. = مسله masla, q.v.
s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

ety: for A. مثلة mas̤alat, n. of un. fr. mas̤al
def: s.m. = mas̤al, q.v.
subs: A rustic proverb; a plain saying.

ety: acc. of mas̤al, q.v.
def: adv. For example, for instance, e.g.; namely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَثَلاً (masalan), from Arabic مَثَلًا (maṯalan).

ety: for A. مسألة
def: s.f. = masala or masla, q.v.

s.m. (pl. of maṣlaḥat, q.v.), lit. 'Things for the good of; or things or affairs conducive to good'; affairs; — employments, occupations; — honour, glory; — materials, ingredients, necessaries (as for cooking, building, &c.); spicery, spices; condiments, seasoning; drug or drugs; materials used to give a finish to a garment, &c.; edging, hem, border; gold or silver lace; — maṣālěḥ-dār, vulg. masāle-dār, adj. & s.m. Seasoned with spices; — bound or edged with lace, &c.; — cloth worked over, or edged, with gold or silver lace, &c.; — one who supplies materials (for a building, &c.)
subs: or maṣālěḥ ḍālnā (-meṅ), To put condiments or seasoning (into), to season.

def: s.m. pl. (of maslak, q.v.), Ways, paths.

ety: masal˚, fr. S. मृश or म्रक्ष, with H. caus. augment al = āl = lā
def: v.t. To rub to pieces; to crush between the palms; to bruise, crush, pulverize, triturate; — to press, squeeze

ety: pl. of masamm, n. of place fr. سمّ 'to pierce, to pass through'; — but used as a sing. in Persian and Urdu
def: s.m. 'Place of passing through,' perforation, or pore (of the body; — pl. masāmmāt).
s.m. The medicinal seed Vernonia (or Serratula) anthelminthica.

ety: Prk. मसाणं; S. श्मशानं
def: s.m. A place where the dead are burnt, crematory, ustrinum (syn. marghaṭ); a cemetery; — a demon, an evil spirit (said to have influence on children); — a disease to which children are liable, possession by an evil spirit; convulsions; — whooping-cough
subs: or sūkhiyā masān, s.m. The whooping-cough.

def: s.m. A species of medicinal plant or fruit, Serratula anthelmintica.
s.m. = مسان masān, q.v.

ety: for A. مثانة mas̤ānat
def: s.m. The bladder
subs: s.m. Weakness of the bladder; inability to retain the urine.

ety: masān, q.v. + Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. One of the (seven) goddesses of small-pox, measles, scarlatina, whooping-cough, &c.

ety: masān, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. One who removes the remains of a burnt corpse; — one who removes the influence of an evil spirit.
s.m. See s.v. mās, 'month.'
s.m. A sapphire; — an emerald.

r: maṣārif, maṣraf, maṣārif-ě-be-jā, maṣārif-ě-shādī, maṣārif-ě-ẓururī, maṣārif-ě-mulk
def: s.m. pl. (of maṣraf, q.v.), Expenses, disbursements; costs; charges; uses; — changes, vicissitudes

ety: for A. مسرة, inf. n. of سَرّ 'to rejoice, to make glad or happy'
def: s.f. A cause of happiness, or joy, or gladness; — joy, happiness, gladness, cheerfulness, liveliness; rapture, delight.
s.f. (dialec.) = mashṭ, q.v.
s.m.f. A kind of bird.

ety: S. मांस+वृद्धिः
def: s.m. A fleshy excrescence or swelling; a wart (syn. massā or masā, q.v.).

ety: pass. part. of ثبت 'to remain fixed,' &c.
def: adj. Fixed, firm.

ety: n. of place fr. صدر 'to return, or go back'
def: s.m. 'Place whence one returns, or goes, or turns away'; place of issuing or proceeding; origin, source, spring, theme; (in Gram.) the root (of a word) from which proceed the derivatives of verbs, an infinitive noun, nomen verbi, or nomen actionis.
adj. Of or relating to the maṣdar or infinitive; having the sense of the verbal noun; — belonging to a source.

ety: pass. part. of سدّ 'to close, stop,' &c.
def: part. Closed, closed up, shut, stopped, obstructed
subs: v.n. To be closed, or stopped, or obstructed; — to stop, cease, fail.

ety: pass. part. of صدر 'to return, or go back,' &c.
def: part. Issued; derived; derivative.

ety: for A. مصدورة maṣdūrat, fem. of maṣdūr
def: part. = maṣdūr, q.v.

ety: pl. of mas (not in use) = Prk. मस्स = S. श्मश्रु
def: s.f. pl. Hair or down on the face which appears in early youth
subs: The hair of the whiskers, &c. to begin to sprout.

def: adj. corr. of مشغول mashg̠ūl, q.v.

ety: S. माशः
def: s.m. (dialec.) Anger.

ety: inf. n. of مسح 'to wipe,' &c.
def: s.m. Wiping; drawing the hand over the surface (of anything, in order to wipe it); handling, stroking, rubbing; washing, cleaning; anointing; the wiping (the neck, &c.) which forms part of the purificatory rites observed by Muslims before engaging in prayer; — perambulation; travelling.

ety: S. माषकः
def: s.m. A kind of bean, &c. ( = māsh, q.v.); — a jeweller's or goldsmith's weight, equal to eight rattīs or seeds of the Abrus precatorius (it is sometimes, however, reckoned = ten rattīs, and sometimes = five; the weight in common use is about 17 grains Troy); — four annas (in the language of brokers); — a small nail
subs: The weight of a māshā, full eight rattis
v.n. To be one moment a māshā the next a tolā; to be very changeable, fickle, or inconstant.

def: s.m. A weight of eight rattīs, &c. ( = māshā, q.v.).

s.m. A kind of bean, Phaseolus radiatus; — a kind of pulse (having seeds marked with black and grey spots; — in Hindī, the name is given to mūng; in Urdū, to mūṅg and to uṛad); a cutaneous eruption resembling beans; — a fool, blockhead
subs: s.f. A kind of leguminous shrub, Glycine debilis
(-ko), To throw beans at; to charm, to bind by a spell.

def: s.m. pl. (of shabah, q.v.), Likenesses, resemblances, semblances; forms, figures, pictures, images.
s.m. pl. (of mashg̠ala, q.v.), Employments, occupations, duties (of a house, &c.).
(pl. of mashhūr, q.v.), adj. & s.m. pl. Renowned, famous, celebrated, notorious; — renowned or famous men; celebrities.
s.m. pl. (of shaiḵẖ, q.v.), Old or aged men; shaiḵẖs; elders; holy persons; doctors; patriarchs; dervishes; — (as a sing.) a holy man; a patriarch, &c.

ety: mashāʼiḵẖ+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. & adv. Of or relating to holy persons, &c.; — patriarchal; — in the manner of holy men; as a spiritual director.
s.f. Holiness, sanctity, piety.
s.m. A bean, &c. ( = māshā, q.v.).

ety: for A. مشقة, inf. n. of شقّ 'to distress; to be distressing; to be difficult,' &c.
def: s.f. Inconvenience, trouble, labour, pains, toil, assiduity; distress, pain, affliction, misfortune
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مشقت (mašaqqat), itself from Arabic مَشَقَّة (mašaqqa).

ety: n. of place fr. شعل 'to burn,' &c.
def: s.f. A sort of cresset; a torch, flambeau; — a lantern
subs: To show a torch, to hold a torch before or near (a person); to carry a lighted torch before (a person)

ety: for A. مشعلة mashʻalat
def: s.m. = mashʻal, q.v.
s.m. A bearer of the mashʻal, a torch-bearer; — (in India, and in the households of Europeans) a scullion.

ety: pl. of mishamm, n. of instr.fr. شمّ 'to smell'
def: s.m. (lit. 'noses'), The smell, the sense of smelling.

ety: v.n. fr. مشر
def: adj. & s.m. Going (or one who goes) with a graceful, sprightly air.

ety: n. of place, or instr. fr. شعر 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. External sense (as hearing or seeing, &c.).

ety: fr. عشرة ʻasharat, 'ten'
def: s.m. A company, troop, collective body (of people), a community, society (ʻso called because they are many').
s.f. (dialec.) = masahrī, q.v.

ety: for A. مشعرة mashʻarat; see mashʻar
def: s.m. A place where poets meet to recite their verses, &c.; a rendezvous of poets.
s.f. See مسہري masahrī.

ety: mashfiq+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adv. Affectionately, kindly; in a friendly manner.

ety: for A. مشغلة mashg̠alat, v.n. fr. شغل 'to busy,' &c.
def: s.m. 'A thing that causes one to be busied,' &c.; business, occupation, employment; — pastime; diversion.

ety: pass. part. of شغل 'to busy, occupy,' &c.
def: part. Busied, busy, occup ed, employed, engaged (in); wholly dedicated (to), diligent; anxious (about)
subs: (-meṅ, or -se), To be busied or busy (with), be occupied (with); to engage (in), &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَشْغُول (mašġūl), from Arabic مَشْغُول (mašḡūl). Compare Punjabi مَشْغُول (maśġūl) / ਮਸ਼ਗ਼ੂਲ (maśġūl), Sindhi مَشْغُول (maśġūl) / मशग़ूल (maśġūl), Gujarati મશગૂલ (maśgūl), Bengali মশগুল (mośgul).

def: s.m. corr. of mashg̠ala, q.v.

ety: fr. mashg̠ūl
def: s.f. Occupation, employment (i.q. mashg̠ala),

ety: n. of place fr. شہد 'to bear witness or testimony' (to
def: s.f. A place where a martyr has died, or is buried; a place of martyrdom; a place of religious visitation; a tomb; — a place of assembling, an assembly.

ety: pass. part. of شہد 'to bear witness or testimony'
def: part. adj. Witnessed; attested; proved; evinced; beheld; — clear, manifest; — s.m. Friday; — the day of resurrection.

ety: pass. part. of شہر 'to make conspicuous, or notorious,' &c.
def: part. adj. Well-known, celebrated, notable, noted, famous, notorious; apparent, conspicuous, manifest, public, published, given out, proclaimed, divulged, reported. — mashhūr karnā or kar-denā, v.t. To give out, publish, proclaim
subs: To be or become famous or notorious as; — to be given out, reported, made public
It is well known; — it is given out, or reported, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مشهور (mašhūr), from Arabic مَشْهُور (mašhūr, “famous, notorious”).

ety: pass. part. of شہر 'tomake conspicuous, or notorious,' &c.
def: part. adj. Well-known, celebrated, notable, noted, famous, notorious; apparent, conspicuous, manifest, public, published, given out, proclaimed, divulged, reported. — mashhūr karnā or kar-denā, v.t. To give out, publish, proclaim
subs: To be or become famous or notorious as; — to be given out, reported, made public
It is well known; — it is given out, or reported, &c.

ety: S. माष+इन्+कः
def: adj. Of māsh, made or consisting of māsh or uṛad; of the colour of māsh
subs: s.m. The colour of māsh or uṛad.

def: s.f. Ink (i.q. masi, or masī, q.v.); — the stalk of the Nyctanthes arbor tristis.

ety: prob. pron. māćhī
def: s.f. (dialec.) = māćhī = maććhī, q.v.

ety: for A. mashyuṉ, inf. n. of مَشَي
def: s.f. Walking, going.

ety: for A. مَشِيْئَة inf. n. of شاءَ, for شيأ
def: s.f. Will, pleasure, wish, desire; will of God, fate.
adj. Sown with beans (māsh); — s.m. A field producing black kidney-beans, a bean-field.

ety: pass. part. of شين 'to disgrace, or dishonour,' &c.
def: part. adj. 'Disgraced, dishonoured'; — villainous; worthless.

ety: for A. مَشِيْئَة inf. n. of شاءَ, for شيأ
def: s.f. Will, pleasure, wish, desire; will of God, fate.

ety: inf. n. of مشق 'to pierce suddenly' (with a spear, &c.
def: s.f. Piercing suddenly (with a spear); — striking, beating, or lashing with velocity; — tearing; — writing, drawing (the letters); — combing; — chafing, or galling the skin (a garment, &c.); — drawing (a garment, or a thong, &c.) every way in order to stretch it; — eating up the best parts (of forage); — a model (for imitation), a copy (to write after); an example; an exercise; — practice, usage, use; — adj. Galled between the thighs (a man); — tall, slender (girl)
subs: s.m. Anything placed under a sheet of paper that one may write commodiously; a paste-board, a pad.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَشْق (mašq), Arabic مَشْق (mašq).

ety: S. मशकः
def: s.f. A leather bag (esp. for carrying water; — the entire hide of a goat or sheep made into a bag and used by water-carriers. In some parts of India the term is applied to an inflated buffalo-hide, which is used as a float in crossing a river)
subs: (-ke nām), To discharge or empty a maśak (in the name of a goddess, or against malign influence); to quench the thirst, or to appease the wrath (of the goddess of small-pox); to remove the influence (of an evil spirit)
(-kī), To have copious discharges (of), to have diarrhœa.

ety: rel. n. fr. mashq, q.v.
def: adj. Acquired by practice; — relating to a copy or an exercise; copied on.

ety: mashk, q.v.+īza, dim. aff. = īća = S. इ+क
def: s.m. A small mashk or leather water-bag; a small leather water-bottle.

ety: pass. part. of شك 'to be dubious, or confused; to doubt,' &c.
def: adj. Doubted; doubtful, uncertain, problematical, ambiguous; — altered, tampered with.

ety: pass. part. of شکر 'to thank, praise,' &c.
def: part. adj. Thanked; praised; acknowledged, rewarded; — worthy of thanks or praise, laudable; agreeable, acceptable; — thankful, grateful (i.q. shukr-guẕār)
subs: v.n. To be thanked; or praised, &c.; — to be thankful or grateful (for, -kā).

ety: fr. mashkūr
def: s.f. Thanking; — praise; — thankfulness; — reward.

ety: pass. part. of شلّ 'to be deprived of the power of motion' (the arm, &c.
def: adj. Dried up, withered (hand, or arm); paralytic.

ety: pass. part. of شمل 'to include in common,' &c.
def: part. Included in common, incorporated (with); comprehended, contained; surrounded; — common, universal (see shāmil).

ety: for A. مشمولة mashmūlat, fem. of mashmūl
def: adj. = mashmūl, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of شمّ 'to smell'
def: part. adj. Smelled; — smelling, perfumed, odoriferous, fragrant; — having a smatch.

ety: inf. n. of مشن
def: s.m. Striking gently (with a sword, or scourge); — scratching, excoriating, grazing (the skin); — lying with; — rubbing, or smoothing (with the hand); — giving.

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, fr. شرب 'to drink,' &c.
def: s.m. Drinking; imbibing; — place of drinking water, drinking-place; a reservoir of water; — (fig.) sect, religion; — nature, disposition, temper, humour.

ety: for A. مشربة mashrabat, n. of un. fr. mashrab, q.v.
def: s.m. A drinking-place ( = mashrab); — a banqueting-room, dining-room, parlour; — a verandah, portico, gallery.
s.f. = masraṅgī, q.v.

ety: n. of place fr. شرق 'to rise' (as the sun
def: s.m. The place or quarter of sunrise, the east, the orient (i.q. sharq).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مشرق, from Arabic مَشْرِق (mašriq).

ety: obl. dual. of mashriq
def: s.m. The place of sunrise and the place of sunset, the east and the west
subs: or dūṛī-ě-mashriqain, s.f. The distance of the east and the west.

ety: rel. n. fr. mashriq
def: adj. Of the east, eastern, oriental (e.g. mashriqī zabān, 'oriental tongue or lauguages'; — mashriqī ḵẖayālāt, 'oriental ideas'; — mashriqī mulk, 'eastern country or countries, the east'; — mashriqī niṣf-kura, 'the eastern hemisphere').

ety: pass. part. of شرع 'to enter upon, begin,' &c.; — to institute, establish, prescribe' (a religious ordinance, or a law, &c.
def: part. Entered upon, begun, commenced, initiated; — instituted, established, prescribed by law, legal, just, comformable to law; — s.m. A kind of cloth of silk and cotton mixed (in which Moḥammadans are allowed to pray; that not being allowed in cloth entirely of silk).
s.m. corr. of مشروع mashrūʻ, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of شرب 'to drink'
def: adj. Drunk; imbibed; — drinkable, potable.

ety: pass. part. of شرح 'to show; to explain,' &c.
def: part. Expounded, explained; declared; illustrated; — above-mentioned, aforesaid.

ety: acc. of mashrūḥ
def: adv. Agreeably to the explanation; circumstantially.

ety: pass. part. of شرط 'to make (a thing
def: part. adj. Made, or imposed as, a condition; conditional; contingent; — agreed upon, stipulated; — defined, limited; — under obligation, pledged; — s.m. A settled revenue of ten per cent. allowed to the zamīndārs

ety: acc. of mashrūt̤
def: adv. Conditionally, &c.
adj. = mashrūt̤, q.v.

ety: intens. n. fr. mashq, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Well-practised, well-versed (in, -meṅ); — one well-practised (in); a practiser; a proficient, an expert.

ety: fr. mashshāq
def: s.f. Exercise, practice; constant or great practice (in anything; syn. mashq).

ety: for A. مشاطة mashshāt̤at, v.n. fr. مشط 'to comb,' &c.
def: s.f. A comber of hair; waiting-maid; tire-woman; bride-dresser; (in Urdu, also) a woman who makes or concerts marriages, a match-maker, a go-between.

ety: abst. s. fr. next, q.v.
def: s.f. The art of attiring (a bride, &c.); — the business of a tire-woman.
s.f. Silence
subs: v.n. To remain silent and still; to feign sleep

def: s.m. A sieve, searce; a bolting or ranging sieve; — skimmer, spattle, ladle; — a sort of coarse woollen manufacture (worn by derwishes or beggars).

ety: pass. part. of صحب 'to accompany,' &c.
def: part. adj. Associated; accompanied; — accompanying.

ety: pass. part. of سحق 'to bruise,' &c.
def: part. Bruised, brayed, pounded, pulverized.
(pass. part. of عشق 'to love passionately'), s.m. = P معشوقه maʻshūqa (for A. معشوقة maʻshūqat, fem. of maʻshūq), s.f. (lit. 'loved, beloved'), A beloved object; beloved, love, sweetheart, mistress.
(for A. معشوقة maʻshūqat, fem. of maʻshūq), s.f. = A معشوق maʻshūq (pass. part. of عشق 'tolove passionately'), s.m. (lit. 'loved, beloved'), A beloved object; beloved, love, sweetheart, mistress.

ety: maʻshūq, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. & adv. Like one beloved; of one beloved; — lovely, charming.

r: maʻshūqī, maʻshūq, ī, i, maʻshūqīyat, īyat
ety: maʻshūq, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. A being loved; the state or condition of a beloved object; — loveliness, charm.

r: maʻshūqīyat, maʻshūq, īyat, maʻshūqī, ī, i
ety: maʻshūq+Ar. abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. A being loved; the state or condition of a beloved object; — loveliness, charm.

ety: pass. part. of سحر 'to enchant, fascinate'
def: part. Bewitched, enchanted, fascinated.

ety: for A. مشورة mashwarat, abst. subst. fr. شاور 'to consult with,' iii of شار (for شور
def: s.m. A criminal conspiracy
subs: (se), To take counsel (with), to consult; to ask (or to take) advice (from).

ety: for A. مشورة mashwarat, abst. subst. fr. شاور 'to consult with,' iii of شار (for شور
def: s.m. A criminal conspiracy
subs: (se), To take counsel (with), to consult; to ask (or to take) advice (from).

def: s.m. A bean-field ( = māshīn).
(for A. مشيخة, pl. of shaiḵẖ, q.v.), s.m. pl. Shaikhs, old men, seniors, elders, &c.

ety: Prk. माउसिआ; S. मातृ+श्वसृका
def: s.f. Mother's sister, aunt ( = mausī).
s.f. Ink. (syn. syāhī)
subs: or masī-dān, or masi-dhān, s.m., and masi-dhānī or masī-dhānī, s.f., and masyādhār (˚si+ādh˚), s.m. An ink-pot, ink-bottle, inkstand.

def: s.f. (dialec.) corr. of masjid, q.v. (cf. masīt).
s.f. See s.v. مسي masi.
s.m. 'The Anointed,' Messiah, Christ

ety: fr. masīḥ
def: s.m. = masīḥ, q.v.
subs: adj. Of the nature or disposition of the Messiah
adj. & s.m. Possessed of the breath of the Messiah; — a skilful physician (as if endowed, like the Messiah, with the power of recovering the sick or recalling the dead by a breath).

ety: rel. n. fr. masīḥā, and masīḥ
def: adj. Of the Messiah; appertaining to Christ; Christian; Christ-like; — s.m. A follower of the Messiah, a Christian
subs: s.f. The office or the works of the Messiah; the miraculous power of the Messiah
To perform Christ-like acts; to heal the sick, to recall the dead to life.

ety: S. मसी+धानी
def: s.f. Ink-pot, inkstand (see masi-dhānī, s.v. masi or masī).

ety: rel. n. fr. masīḥā, and masīḥ
def: adj. Of the Messiah; appertaining to Christ; Christian; Christ-like; — s.m. A follower of the Messiah, a Christian
subs: s.f. The office or the works of the Messiah; the miraculous power of the Messiah
To perform Christ-like acts; to heal the sick, to recall the dead to life.

def: adj. Relating or pertaining to a month; monthly; happening every month; lasting or continuing for a month; payable in a month; hired by the month; falling at the end of a month; — s.m. A monthly servant; monthly wages, hire, or stipend; — a śrādh or obsequial sacrifice performed monthly (for a twelvemonth) on the recurrence of the lunar day of the death of a relative
subs: s.m. A paymaster (esp. of monthly servants).

def: s.f. Monthly wages, or hire, or stipend.
s.f. Monthly wages, or hire, or stipend.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), One month old; monthly; — any creature a month old.

ety: S. मसीनः and मसीन+ कः
def: s.m. A vetch; pulse (a general name for such pulse as mūng, ahṛaṛ, &c.; syn. dalihan); — linseed, Linum usitatissimum.

ety: S. मसीनः and मसीन+ कः
def: s.m. A vetch; pulse (a general name for such pulse as mūng, ahṛaṛ, &c.; syn. dalihan); — linseed, Linum usitatissimum.
s.f. masīn, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of سير 'to go, journey,' &c.
def: s.f. Going, walking; proceeding on a jonrney, travelling (syn. sair).
s.f. (dialec.) corr. of masjid, q.v.
see s.v. mā, 'what.'

ety: for A. معصية, inf. n. of عصا (for عصی
def: s.f. Disobedience; rebellion; defection; — sin.
